source: Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) interim measures on medical and health insurance/takaful (IPMI) repricing, announced December 2024; and Nov 2024 Malaysian industry/press reports on the 2024-25 repricing cycle (insurers sought one-step premium increases of +40% to +70% driven by ~19.6% medical claims inflation). The RM250/month base premium and the 10-year glide it feeds are an ILLUSTRATIVE projection of the BNM-capped <=10%/yr mechanism, not a quoted premium for any specific policy.
verification_status: mixed: verified (BNM Dec 2024 measures + Nov 2024 40-70% repricing context) / derived (the RM250/month illustrative 10%/yr glide)
notes: Two kinds of facts live here, kept strictly separate. (A) VERIFIED regulatory/market context — in the 2024-25 IPMI cycle insurers SOUGHT one-step premium increases of +40% to +70% (driven by medical claims inflation ~19.6%); BNM's Dec 2024 interim measures FORBID that one-off pass-through and instead require increases to be STAGGERED over a minimum of 3 years (through end-2026), with at least 80% of policyholders facing annual premium adjustments of NO MORE THAN 10% per year. Under a sustained 10%/yr cap, a premium roughly DOUBLES every ~7.3 years (ln2 / ln1.10 = 7.27). This context is documented below in regulatory_context but is NOT plotted as a point — the 40-70% never appears in the series, because BNM capped exactly that one-off jump. (B) DERIVED illustrative glide — to depict what a monthly premium does UNDER the regulated mechanism, we take an illustrative RM250/month medical-insurance premium today and apply only the BNM-capped <=10%/yr glide: RM250 x 1.10^t for years 0..10, reaching ~RM648/month at year 10. There is NO year-1 spike: modelling a +50% one-off step would contradict the very BNM cap this dataset cites. The RM250 base is illustrative and not a quoted premium; actual premiums, repricing magnitude, and per-policyholder caps vary by insurer, plan, age and claims experience. The early-report 'age-60 pause' is deliberately OMITTED — it is not in the confirmed BNM measure text. Currency: RM (Malaysian ringgit), nominal ringgit of each year. Illustrative model output — not personalised advice, not a guaranteed or assured result. This depicts insurance PREMIUM inflation (what you pay), distinct from medical-COST inflation (the price of treatment).

regulatory_context.insurers_sought_one_step_increase_note: Documented context only — the magnitude insurers SOUGHT before regulation. NOT a plotted point: BNM forbade the one-off pass-through, so this range never enters the series.
citations: Bank Negara Malaysia — interim measures on medical and health insurance/takaful repricing, December 2024
Nov 2024 Malaysian industry/press reports — insurers sought IPMI one-step premium hikes of 40-70% driven by ~19.6% claims inflation

model_assumptions.glide_basis: BNM <=10%/yr cap for >=80% of policyholders, staggered over >=3 years (no one-off step)
